body.title: [sprint] Calibre device: machine config -> ~/.unseen_university/devices/calibre/; fix library path; wire loader
body.intention: I intend that the calibre device's only path data is machine-specific runtime config in ~/.unseen_university/devices/calibre/, so the repo code is portable and the next installer's paths never collide with Akien's.
body.description:
**Problem:** the calibre device bakes Akien's machine path into the repo TWO ways, both WRONG: (a) a hardcoded default in device.py (_LIBRARY_PATH_DEFAULT) and (b) a repo-committed config.cfg — and config.cfg is DEAD (device.py never reads it; docstring references 'config/calibre.cfg' but no loader exists). Both point at '/media/akien/onedrive/AkiensMedia/Ebooks/Calibre Library', which is MISSING the 'Calibre Portable' segment — the real library is at '/media/akien/onedrive/AkiensMedia/Ebooks/Calibre Portable/Calibre Library' (verified: metadata.db present there). Machine-specific data does not belong in repo code (it's about Akien's computer, not the next installer's). **Proposed shape:** (1) WIRE a config loader that reads library_path + calibredb_path from `uu_home()/devices/calibre/config.cfg` (the established ~/.unseen_university/devices/<device>/ convention — cc_0, granny_weatherwax already live there); env (CALIBRE_LIBRARY_PATH/CALIBREDB_PATH) still overrides first; constructor kwargs still win for tests. (2) SEED the runtime config file with the CORRECT path (…/Ebooks/Calibre Portable/Calibre Library). (3) REMOVE the repo-committed unseen_university/devices/calibre/config.cfg (machine data out of the repo); if a template is wanted, ship config.cfg.example with a placeholder, not a real path. (4) DROP the Akien-specific hardcoded default from device.py — the code default must be non-machine-specific (empty/None -> clear 'configure me' error, or a documented placeholder), never a real user's path. **Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/calibre/device.py; DELETE unseen_university/devices/calibre/config.cfg; create ~/.unseen_university/devices/calibre/config.cfg (runtime, not repo). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE import (calibredb subprocess only — the sanctioned external-DB exception); no machine-specific paths in repo; external-state principle (device config in runtime dir). **Scope boundary:** IN: config loader + runtime file + path fix + remove repo config.cfg + de-hardcode code default. OUT: callers-over-bus migration (T-calibre-books-over-bus); adding new MCP tools. **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) with a temp uu_home containing devices/calibre/config.cfg, CalibreDevice() loads that library_path; (2) env override beats the config file; (3) grep shows NO '/media/akien' or Akien-specific path in unseen_university/devices/calibre/*.py or a repo config.cfg; (4) calibredb --with-library on the seeded path resolves (search returns for a known title, when calibredb present). **Completion criteria:** device reads machine config from ~/.unseen_university/devices/calibre/; correct path (Calibre Portable segment); repo carries no machine path; env override intact; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
